name: Professor Fiona B Adamson
title: Professor of International Relations
department: Department of Politics and International Studies
expertise_topics: Fiona's current research projects include a British Academy-funded project on the Diplomacy of Forced Migration (with Kelly M. Greenhill and Jente Althuis). The project will produce a dataset on the diplomatic and foreign policy dimensions of state-organized involuntary migration
ranging from historical cases of population exchanges and transfers to contemporary practices of forced returns and repatriation
she is working on several diaspora-related projects
including a comparative study of diasporic geopolitics and the role of diaspora mobilization in anti-colonial and separatist conflicts. In the context of her EU H2020 funded project
she is leading a study of Kurdish refugees
migrants and diaspora organisations in Europe
examining the geopolitical and diasporic aspects of refugeehood
Fiona has a longstanding interest in the relationship between migration
mobility and contestations over nation- and state-building
Identity Politics in Turkey and Its Diaspora(s)
research_interests: Fiona's current research projects include a British Academy-funded project on the Diplomacy of Forced Migration (with Kelly M. Greenhill and Jente Althuis). The project will produce a dataset on the diplomatic and foreign policy dimensions of state-organized involuntary migration; ranging from historical cases of population exchanges and transfers to contemporary practices of forced returns and repatriation; In addition; she is working on several diaspora-related projects; including a comparative study of diasporic geopolitics and the role of diaspora mobilization in anti-colonial and separatist conflicts. In the context of her EU H2020 funded project; she is leading a study of Kurdish refugees; migrants and diaspora organisations in Europe; examining the geopolitical and diasporic aspects of refugeehood; More generally; Fiona has a longstanding interest in the relationship between migration; mobility and contestations over nation- and state-building; Ph; D Supervision Areas; Migration and International Relations; Diaspora Politics; Politics of Forced Migration; Migration Diplomacy; Civil War and Political Violence; Identity Politics in Turkey and Its Diaspora(s); Algeria and France; Colonial Histories and Conflict; Non-State Actors and Transnationalism; Exile
